summary: A brief woodworking tip about using masking tape on inside corners before gluing to prevent messy squeeze-out. Shapiro recommends masking off hard-to-clean areas, letting the glue set for 30-45 minutes, then peeling off the tape for clean results. The post is a quick practical maker tip with no broader argument.
key_points: Masking tape on inside corners before gluing prevents hard-to-clean squeeze-out
Wait 30-45 minutes before peeling off the tape and excess glue
Plan for cleanup difficulty before assembly, not after
money_quotes: When gluing up something that will be hard to clean when complete, use masking tape to keep the squeezeout from messing up your project.
key_insight: Prevent glue mess on hard-to-reach joints by masking off corners with tape before assembly.
